codeReview.summary: The fix correctly moves cache invalidation calls to immediately after successful PromoteMember operations in both demote() and promote() functions. This ensures the admin cache is invalidated only when the Telegram API operation succeeds, preventing stale cache scenarios where the operation succeeds but subsequent steps (GetMember, SetAdministratorCustomTitle) fail. The change follows the established pattern of cache invalidation immediately after successful DB/API operations as seen in other modules like db/filters_db.go, db/blacklists_db.go, and db/greetings_db.go.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The codebase consistently follows the pattern of invalidating cache immediately after successful write operations. Examples: db/filters_db.go:86, db/blacklists_db.go:28, db/greetings_db.go:173, db/warns_db.go:134. The fix aligns with this pattern.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: alita/db/filters_db.go:85-86 - 'Invalidate cache after adding filter'; alita/db/blacklists_db.go:27-28 - 'Invalidate cache after adding blacklist'; alita/db/greetings_db.go:172-173 - 'Invalidate cache after updating welcome text'

summary: Cache invalidation timing fix in admin.go is correct and follows established patterns. The cache is now invalidated immediately after successful PromoteMember operations in both demote() (line 228) and promote() (line 413) functions, ensuring cache consistency even when subsequent operations fail. Tests pass with race detection enabled.
